blocks.content: Daniel looked at him. Something recalculated.
He stepped aside. Just like that.

blocks.speaker: Daniel
blocks.content: Tell him Daniel said what's up.
He walked off. Larry kept moving. A sophomore who'd been nearby caught Larry's eye and gave him a look — half impressed, half something else.
He told himself that was the smart play. Use what you have. Nothing wrong with that. The part he didn't say out loud was that it felt like borrowing something that wasn't his.

blocks.prompt: What does Larry tell himself walking to class?

options.label: That was the right call. You played it smart.
You used his name. You didn't earn that.
Jonathan would've done the same thing.
